title: cgroup behavior is not consistent
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**
cgroup behavior is not consistent in my environment
```
root@comports1:/sys/fs/cgroup# cat /etc/lsb-release 
DISTRIB_ID=Ubuntu
DISTRIB_RELEASE=20.04
DISTRIB_CODENAME=focal
DISTRIB_DESCRIPTION="Ubuntu 20.04.3 LTS"
```
<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
Ubuntu
1. put current shell into a cgroup
```
lsfadmin@comports1:~$ echo $$
7568
```
```
root@comports1:/sys/fs/cgroup/test# cat cgroup.procs 
7568
```
2. run a container with DBUS_SESSION_BUS_ADDRESS. The configured path cannot be accessed by rootless podman
```
lsfadmin@comports1:~$ env | grep DBUS
DBUS_SESSION_BUS_ADDRESS=unix:path=/run/user/0/bus
lsfadmin@comports1:~$ podman run --rm --cgroup-manager cgroupfs ubuntu sleep 8888
```

all podman related processes are here in the cgroup
```
root@comports1:/sys/fs/cgroup/test# cat cgroup.procs 
7568
522712
522747
522787
522788
522791
522794
```
3. run a container with no DBUS_SESSION_BUS_ADDRESS
```
lsfadmin@comports1:~$ unset DBUS_SESSION_BUS_ADDRESS
lsfadmin@comports1:~$ podman run --rm --cgroup-manager cgroupfs ubuntu sleep 8888
```
```
root@comports1:/sys/fs/cgroup/test# cat cgroup.procs 
7568
523116
```
other processes, for example 'conmon', are not in the current cgroup.
```
root@comports1:/sys/fs/cgroup# ps -ef | grep ad0
lsfadmin  523203       1  0 01:27 ?        00:00:00 /usr/libexec/podman/conmon --api-version 1 -c ad08f45002c9fb67460789c77273bcce06b0c67e0e34d3e06b2648c4e84ca5e1 -u ad08f45002c9fb67460789c77273bcce06b0c67e0e34d3e06b2648c4e84ca5e1 -r /usr/bin/crun -b /home/lsfadmin/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay-containers/ad08f45002c9fb67460789c77273bcce06b0c67e0e34d3e06b2648c4e84ca5e1/userdata -p /run/user/1000/containers/overlay-containers/ad08f45002c9fb67460789c77273bcce06b0c67e0e34d3e06b2648c4e84ca5e1/userdata/pidfile -n cranky_liskov --exit-dir /run/user/1000/libpod/tmp/exits --full-attach -l journald --log-level warning --runtime-arg --log-format=json --runtime-arg --log --runtime-arg=/run/user/1000/containers/overlay-containers/ad08f45002c9fb67460789c77273bcce06b0c67e0e34d3e06b2648c4e84ca5e1/userdata/oci-log --conmon-pidfile /run/user/1000/containers/overlay-containers/ad08f45002c9fb67460789c77273bcce06b0c67e0e34d3e06b2648c4e84ca5e1/userdata/conmon.pid --exit-command /usr/bin/podman --exit-command-arg --root --exit-command-arg /home/lsfadmin/.local/share/containers/storage --exit-command-arg --runroot --exit-command-arg /run/user/1000/containers --exit-command-arg --log-level --exit-command-arg warning --exit-command-arg --cgroup-manager --exit-command-arg cgroupfs --exit-command-arg --tmpdir --exit-command-arg /run/user/1000/libpod/tmp --exit-command-arg --runtime --exit-command-arg crun --exit-command-arg --storage-driver --exit-command-arg overlay --exit-command-arg --storage-opt --exit-command-arg overlay.mount_program=/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs --exit-command-arg --events-backend --exit-command-arg journald --exit-command-arg container --exit-command-arg cleanup --exit-command-arg --rm --exit-command-arg ad08f45002c9fb67460789c77273bcce06b0c67e0e34d3e06b2648c4e84ca5e1
root      523434  518327  0 01:28 pts/6    00:00:00 grep --color=auto ad0
root@comports1:/sys/fs/cgroup# find /sys/fs/cgroup/ -name cgroup.procs | xargs grep 523203
/sys/fs/cgroup/user.slice/user-1000.slice/user@1000.service/user.slice/podman-523152.scope/cgroup.procs:523203
```

**Describe the results you received:**
```
root@comports1:/sys/fs/cgroup/test# cat cgroup.procs 
7568
523116
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
```
root@comports1:/sys/fs/cgroup/test# cat cgroup.procs 
7568
523116
523203
523206
...
```
 1. If above display is got, it is perfect for me.
 2. if not, I mean, the step 2 case is a bug instead of step 3, I'd like to know how to make processes of a podman container located in my cgroup as step 2 did.
 3. if all process in one cgroup directory is not a design behavior, then what is the target design to make every processes traceable from cgroup structure.


By the way, I tested the case on cgroup v1, it has similar issues. 


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```

      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +CRIU +YAJL
  os: linux

  security:

    capabilities: CAP_CHOWN,CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE,CAP_FOWNER,CAP_FSETID,CAP_KILL,CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE,CAP_SETFCAP,CAP_SETGID,CAP_SETPCAP,CAP_SETUID,CAP_SYS_CHROOT

  slirp4netns:

    package: 'slirp4netns: /usr/bin/slirp4netns'
    version: |-
      slirp4netns version 1.1.8
      commit: unknown
      libslirp: 4.3.1-git
      SLIRP_CONFIG_VERSION_MAX: 3
      libseccomp: 2.4.3
  swapFree: 21473517568
  swapTotal: 21474828288
  uptime: 176h 27m 48.75s (Approximately 7.33 days)

  log:
  - k8s-file
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
(paste your output here)
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
(paste your output here)
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes/No

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body:
rootless Podman cannot use cgroups with cgroupfs, so there are no new cgroups created.

When running with systemd, rootless Podman is able to create cgroups and move processes around.

@giuseppe 

Thanks for your quick response.

So, if rootless cannot use cgroupfs (I assume it is technical issue that cannot operate on cgroup), it does not create new cgroup. It is what I expected in my case. Is that a designed behavior? I mean, specify the cgroupfs for rootless podman and stop creating new cgroups.

( If not, what is the official way to stop creating new cgroups for the podman. )

with rootless it is just a necessity.  We cannot handle cgroups if we are not on systemd, or if it is not already manually configured and then you can force it with `--cgroup-parent=`.

If you want to disable creating new cgroups, you can try `--cgroup-manager=disabled`.  It is supported only by crun though.

@giuseppe 
Thanks. It is clear to me now.
